tweets.text:
solana:So11111111111111111111111111111111111111112 | Solana Market Analysis | Weekly/Daily/Hourly

1W: been stuck in range of weekly candle from first week of February for five months now, range lows 67.5 range highs 106

failed to breakdown during high volume selloff + peak fear last month & reclaimed range, now retesting key weekly resistance level 81.5-88

1D: volume profile of the range shows majority of the action has happened between 78-92, w/ POC around 85, close to where we topped out at on this rally, the inefficient down move from 83 to 60 has been fully filled, on daily believe closes through June highs @ 83 would push us back to range highs, usually when you fail to breakout to one side of the range and reclaim you end up retesting the opposite side of the range

am betting on us forming a higher low here in the mid 70s but believe price action will be choppy until we cleanly trade above the topside of this range > 106

good place to accumulate spot position for long term with weekly dollar cost avg buys, inval if we lose the lows from June

1H: retesting low TF demand at beginning of Q3 before breaking mtf range to upside, believe good spot to look for longs 72-75 w/ invals < 71 going into next week expecting retest of 83-85, extended targets 106 & then 150+ later in Q3
tweets.full_text:
solana:So11111111111111111111111111111111111111112 | Solana Market Analysis | Weekly/Daily/Hourly

1W: been stuck in range of weekly candle from first week of February for five months now, range lows 67.5 range highs 106

failed to breakdown during high volume selloff + peak fear last month & reclaimed range, now retesting key weekly resistance level 81.5-88

1D: volume profile of the range shows majority of the action has happened between 78-92, w/ POC around 85, close to where we topped out at on this rally, the inefficient down move from 83 to 60 has been fully filled, on daily believe closes through June highs @ 83 would push us back to range highs, usually when you fail to breakout to one side of the range and reclaim you end up retesting the opposite side of the range

am betting on us forming a higher low here in the mid 70s but believe price action will be choppy until we cleanly trade above the topside of this range > 106

good place to accumulate spot position for long term with weekly dollar cost avg buys, inval if we lose the lows from June

1H: retesting low TF demand at beginning of Q3 before breaking mtf range to upside, believe good spot to look for longs 72-75 w/ invals < 71 going into next week expecting retest of 83-85, extended targets 106 & then 150+ later in Q3

tweets.text:
Reminder that the $SPX is only down 2% from ATH’s. Barely scraping the definition of a “run of the mill” chart below:

-2% to -3% is a run of the mill week to week move.

-5% to -7% is a pullback.

-10% is a correction.

-15% is a deeper correction.

-20% to -25% is a bear market.

-30% to -40% is a severe bear market.

-50% is a crash.

-60% is a severe crash.

-70%+ is welcome to hell.

Three weeks ago I said take a look at the volume in $MSFT.

Highest daily volume in 12 yrs.

Highest weekly volume in 6 yrs.

Highest monthly volume in 6 yrs.

It marked A bottom (for now) and rewarded a 15% pretty unabated bounce since.

All at the same time respecting the 50mma, the 50wma, and now it’s hunting for the 50dma.

I like this name if it can close above $400 (where the 50dma is, came close the other day) to head towards the 200dma around $430ish then.

tweets.text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours: too deep into the escalation trap or the cycle of stupid.

The events over the past week should start to dissuade anyone from contemplating that the Strait of Hormuz is going back to normal anytime soon. The video from @MarineTraffic illustrates that clearly with Iran's lane being the only active one.

Today, IRGC said that 4 vessels were halted, while the US is saying that's fake. No one knows what's real anymore, but what is real is that shipowners will be reluctant to take the risk. He said, she said isn't good enough anymore when lives are at risk.

We are now firmly in @ProfessorPape's escalation trap and @MalcolmNance's cycle of stupid. It is clear and obvious the US cannot give Iran the control of the Strait of Hormuz because it will make it the most powerful oil producer in the world. And since this conflict is all or nothing, we will either have to topple the regime, or Iran will come out victorious. There's no in-between.

Let's see how this weekend goes, but from a flow perspective, nothing is going through. Zero barrels went through on July 17 and the last two days have not been revised higher by Kpler.
tweets.full_text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours: too deep into the escalation trap or the cycle of stupid.

The events over the past week should start to dissuade anyone from contemplating that the Strait of Hormuz is going back to normal anytime soon. The video from @MarineTraffic illustrates that clearly with Iran's lane being the only active one.

Today, IRGC said that 4 vessels were halted, while the US is saying that's fake. No one knows what's real anymore, but what is real is that shipowners will be reluctant to take the risk. He said, she said isn't good enough anymore when lives are at risk.

We are now firmly in @ProfessorPape's escalation trap and @MalcolmNance's cycle of stupid. It is clear and obvious the US cannot give Iran the control of the Strait of Hormuz because it will make it the most powerful oil producer in the world. And since this conflict is all or nothing, we will either have to topple the regime, or Iran will come out victorious. There's no in-between.

Let's see how this weekend goes, but from a flow perspective, nothing is going through. Zero barrels went through on July 17 and the last two days have not been revised higher by Kpler.
